skill.name: auth
skill.description: Guides implementation of authentication with Clerk or Supabase Auth, and payments with Stripe.
skill.summary: Build secure login, account, subscription, and checkout flows with common SaaS providers.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography and entropy findings are false positives from front matter and Japanese documentation, with no evidence of encoded payloads or weak crypto implementation. Static Ruby backtick findings are false positives from markdown fences, but the skill does allow Bash while guiding authentication and payment changes. This creates elevated operational risk and should be published with a security warning.

medium_findings.title: Bash Access in Sensitive Auth and Payment Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash while directing package installation and authentication or Stripe payment implementation. This is legitimate for setup work, but it increases risk because commands may affect dependencies, environment setup, or application security controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly allow Bash, and the docs describe installing packages and configuring auth or payment flows. I found no malicious command payload, so this is an elevated capability risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto hits occur in descriptions that mention authentication providers, not in cryptographic code. The high-entropy hits are consistent with Japanese text and metadata, and the backtick execution hits are markdown code fences used for display text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show YAML descriptions and markdown fences, not executable crypto or shell code. No evidence found for obfuscated binary content or Ruby backtick execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: Bash is listed as an allowed tool across the skill and its subskills. This capability should be monitored because the skill operates in authentication and payment contexts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command capability is explicit in the allowed-tools metadata. The risk is contextual because no suspicious command string was found.

content.value_statement: Authentication and payment work often fails when setup steps are missed. This skill guides implementation with Clerk, Supabase Auth, and Stripe using focused security checklists.
actual_capabilities: Routes authentication requests to Clerk or Supabase Auth guidance.
Routes payment requests to Stripe subscription or checkout guidance.
Lists security checks for passwords, sessions, CSRF, and rate limits.
Guides login, signup, social login, email verification, and password reset work.
Guides Stripe checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and customer portal setup.
Warns users to avoid storing card data on application servers.
limitations: Does not include complete framework-specific implementation code.
Requires project context before provider-specific steps can be applied.
Does not replace security review for production authentication or payments.
May run package installation commands if the host assistant permits Bash.

use_cases.title: Add Login to a SaaS App
use_cases.description: Use Clerk or Supabase Auth to add signup, login, protected routes, and profile basics.

use_cases.title: Launch Subscription Billing
use_cases.description: Use Stripe to add recurring plans, checkout, webhook handling, and customer portal access.

use_cases.title: Review Security Requirements
use_cases.description: Check authentication and payment tasks for common controls before implementation begins.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Login
prompt_templates.prompt: Add email login to this app using Clerk or Supabase Auth. Review the project first and explain the required setup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Social Sign-In
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Google and GitHub login to the existing authentication flow. Include protected route handling and environment variable guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Add Stripe Checkout
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Stripe checkout for a one-time purchase. Keep prices server-controlled and include webhook verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Subscription Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement subscription billing with Stripe and gate premium routes by active plan status. Include webhook, portal, and failure-state handling.

output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the app structure, selects the Supabase Auth flow, lists required packages, and outlines login, signup, and protected route changes.
The assistant identifies monthly and yearly plan needs, creates a setup plan for checkout and webhooks, and highlights server-side price control.

output_examples.input: Check whether my auth plan is safe.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a focused checklist covering password storage, session cookies, CSRF, rate limits, errors, and sensitive logging.

best_practices: Confirm the target provider before changing application code.
Keep secrets in environment variables and never place them in client code.
Review webhook signatures and server-side authorization before release.
anti_patterns: Do not store raw passwords, card numbers, or secret keys in the application database.
Do not trust client-provided prices, plan names, or payment status.
Do not publish auth or payment changes without a focused security review.

faq.question: Which providers does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Clerk, Supabase Auth, and Stripe based on the included documentation.

faq.question: Can it build both login and payments?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes authentication work to auth guidance and payment work to Stripe guidance.

faq.question: Does it handle password security?
faq.answer: It includes checklist guidance for password hashing, sessions, CSRF, rate limits, and error messages.

faq.question: Does it store card data?
faq.answer: No. The guidance says card data should be handled by Stripe or another payment provider.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because Bash is allowed in sensitive auth and payment workflows.

faq.question: Does it replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No. Production authentication and payment systems still need expert review before launch.
